skill.name: product-marketing-context
skill.description: Creates and maintains a reusable product marketing context document for positioning, audiences, competitors, proof points, messaging, and customer language.
skill.summary: Builds a structured product marketing context file for future marketing work.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static findings for external commands, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, the .md extension, and ordinary marketing text. The skill has a legitimate low-risk filesystem capability because it reads project materials and writes `.claude/product-marketing-context.md`.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Read and Write Capability
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to check for an existing context file, read repository materials, and save `.claude/product-marketing-context.md`. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should understand it may read local project content and create or update a local file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly instruct local file checks, repository reading, and saving a Markdown file. No network transfer, shell execution, or secret collection is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns are not supported by the Markdown context. The cited lines contain inline file paths, a fenced Markdown template, product marketing fields, and normal prose rather than executable code or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows Markdown formatting and marketing template text only. There is no Ruby code, shell command, hashing algorithm, system probe, or prompt injection attempt.

content.value_statement: Marketing work often slows down when positioning, audience, competitors, and customer language are scattered. This skill creates one structured context document that Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can reuse across marketing tasks.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes existing context and asks which sections need updates.
Drafts context from repository materials such as README files, landing pages, package metadata, and documentation.
Guides users through product overview, audience, personas, pain points, competitors, differentiation, objections, and proof points.
Creates a Markdown context document that other marketing skills can reference.
Encourages verbatim customer language instead of generic messaging.
limitations: It depends on available repository content and user-provided corrections.
It does not verify competitor claims or customer proof points independently.
It may need sections skipped or adapted for simple B2C products.
It creates a context document but does not publish marketing assets.

use_cases.title: Create a Shared Marketing Brief
use_cases.description: Build a single source of truth for positioning, customer segments, proof points, and messaging before writing campaigns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Messaging Inputs
use_cases.description: Capture product details, objections, competitors, and customer language so future content starts from consistent context.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI Marketing Workflows
use_cases.description: Create the context file that downstream Claude, Codex, and Claude Code marketing skills can reuse automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Context File
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product marketing context document for this project. Start by checking whether one already exists, then guide me through the recommended next step.

prompt_templates.title: Draft From Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Auto-draft my product marketing context from the README, docs, landing page copy, package metadata, and other visible project materials. Show gaps that need my input.

prompt_templates.title: Update Positioning Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing product marketing context and help me update the target audience, pain points, competitive landscape, and differentiation sections.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Customer Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the customer language and brand voice sections using verbatim phrases I provide. Keep the context document practical for future marketing outputs.

output_examples.input: I need to set up marketing context for my SaaS product.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks for an existing context file, offers auto-draft or guided setup, then starts collecting product overview, audience, pain points, and goals.

output_examples.input: Use this repo to create a first draft.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews available project materials, drafts each context section, identifies uncertain claims, and asks what should be corrected or added.
The assistant summarizes the current entries, asks focused questions about alternatives and sales pushback, then updates only the relevant sections.

best_practices: Provide real customer phrases, sales notes, and support language when available.
Review competitor and proof point sections carefully before reusing them.
Update the context file whenever positioning, pricing, audience, or goals change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the first auto-draft as verified market research.
Do not include confidential customer data unless the workspace is approved for it.
Do not skip user review when the repository has sparse marketing information.

faq.question: What file does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates `.claude/product-marketing-context.md` in the project workspace.

faq.question: Can it draft context without my input?
faq.answer: It can draft from repository materials, but user review is needed to correct gaps and assumptions.

faq.question: Does it work for B2C products?
faq.answer: Yes. Some B2B sections, such as buying personas, can be skipped or adapted.

faq.question: Does it research competitors online?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill performs web research. It focuses on local materials and user input.

faq.question: How do other marketing skills use the result?
faq.answer: They can reference the saved context document to reuse positioning, audience, voice, and proof points.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare customer language, competitor notes, proof points, pricing details, and current business goals.
